planned_changes.description: Export computed result sheets to CSV (one CSV with record-level computed columns and one CSV summarizing sensitivity runs for nominal/+5%/-5%) and include them in [EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT]

planned_changes.change: Embed formula text and cell references in disposition.md
planned_changes.description: For each computed column (severity, monotone fit, residual, tol, flags), add the exact Excel formula or the sheet!cell references so a verifier can map table entries back to workbook cells.

planned_changes.change: Add sensitivity table and exported results
planned_changes.description: Run and export the sensitivity analysis (limits +/-5%) as a compact table showing fit/residual/flag counts side-by-side; include both CSV and an in-markdown rendered table.

planned_changes.change: Ensure openpyxl compatibility and add verification script
planned_changes.description: Modify build_artifacts.py output to use constructs that openpyxl/pandas can read; add a short Python script (requirements: openpyxl,pandas) that reads the workbook or CSVs and recomputes the disposition summary and assertions used by the grader.
Document the generation process, runtime dependencies, and any fallback choices (e.g., manual XML writer) so verifiers understand how artifacts were produced.

raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Improve verifier reproducibility and reduce dependency on environment-specific spreadsheet previews by adding explicit exports, formula/cell references, sensitivity tables, and a verification script; ensure workbook compatibility with common readers (openpyxl).

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline attempt passes artifact-contract and semantic grounding checks. Disposition.md documents method, per-record evidence, monotone running-max fit, residuals, sensitivity notes, and recommendation. Verifier flagged no issues but noted inability to preview the xlsx binary due to missing openpyxl in the grading environment. Recommended improvements focus on verifier-friendliness and reproducibility (CSV/formula dump, explicit cell addresses, sensitivity tables, and ensuring openpyxl-compatible workbook constructs).
